title: Git
description: Complete version control reference from beginner to advanced

commands.name: Install & Configure Git
rows:
john@pc:~$ git config --global user.name "John Doe"
(no output, config saved)

john@pc:~$ git config --global user.email "john@example.com"
(no output, config saved)

john@pc:~$ git config --list
user.name=John Doe
user.email=john@example.com
core.editor=nano
color.ui=auto

john@pc:~$ git config --global core.editor "code --wait"
(no output, config saved)

commands.name: SSH Key Setup
rows:
john@pc:~$ ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C "john@example.com"
Generating public/private rsa key pair.
Enter file in which to save the key (/home/john/.ssh/id_rsa): 
Enter passphrase (empty for no passphrase): 
Enter same passphrase again: 
Your identification has been saved in /home/john/.ssh/id_rsa
Your public key has been saved in /home/john/.ssh/id_rsa.pub

john@pc:~$ ssh -T git@github.com
Hi JohnDoe! You've successfully authenticated, but GitHub does not provide shell access.

commands.name: Create First Repository
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git init
Initialized empty Git repository in /home/john/myproject/.git/

john@pc:~$ git init myrepo
Initialized empty Git repository in /home/john/myrepo/.git/

john@pc:~$ git init --bare repo.git
Initialized empty Git repository in /home/john/repo.git/

john@pc:~/myproject$ ls -la .git
total 12
drwxr-xr-x 7 john john 4096 May 14 10:00 .
drwxr-xr-x 3 john john 4096 May 14 10:00 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john   23 May 14 10:00 HEAD
drwxr-xr-x 2 john john 4096 May 14 10:00 config
drwxr-xr-x 4 john john 4096 May 14 10:00 objects
drwxr-xr-x 2 john john 4096 May 14 10:00 refs

commands.name: Status & Add
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
On branch main
No commits yet

Untracked files:
  (use "git add <file>..." to include)
	index.html
	style.css

john@pc:~/myproject$ git status -s
 M README.md
?? newfile.txt

john@pc:~/myproject$ git add index.html
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status -s
A  index.html
?? style.css

john@pc:~/myproject$ git add .
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
Changes to be committed:
	new file:   index.html
	new file:   style.css

john@pc:~/myproject$ git add -p
Stage this hunk [y,n,q,a,d,e,?]? y
(interactive hunk selection)
Delete file and stage the removal

john@pc:~/myproject$ git rm file.txt
rm 'file.txt'
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
Changes to be committed:
	deleted:    file.txt
Remove from Git, keep on disk

john@pc:~/myproject$ git rm --cached file.txt
john@pc:~/myproject$ ls file.txt
file.txt (still exists on disk)

john@pc:~/myproject$ git mv old.txt new.txt
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
Changes to be committed:
	renamed:    old.txt -> new.txt

commands.name: Commit
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git commit -m "Initial commit"
[main (root-commit) a1b2c3d] Initial commit
 2 files changed, 45 insertions(+)
 create mode 100644 index.html
 create mode 100644 style.css

john@pc:~/myproject$ git commit -a -m "Fix typo"
[main d4e5f6g] Fix typo
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

john@pc:~/myproject$ git commit --amend -m "Better msg"
[main a1b2c3d] Better msg
 Date: Thu May 14 10:00:00 2026
 2 files changed, 45 insertions(+)
Include staged changes in last commit

john@pc:~/myproject$ git commit --amend --no-edit
[main a1b2c3d] Initial commit
 Date: Thu May 14 10:00:00 2026
 3 files changed, 50 insertions(+)
Force a commit with no changes

john@pc:~/myproject$ git commit --allow-empty -m "CI trigger"
[main 9i0j1k2] CI trigger

commands.name: Log & Show
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git log --oneline
a1b2c3d Initial commit
b2c3d4e Add README
c3d4e5f Fix typo

john@pc:~/myproject$ git log --oneline --graph --all
* a1b2c3d (HEAD -> main) Initial commit
| * d4e5f6g (feature) Add feature
|/  
* c3d4e5f Fix typo
Show actual changes in each commit

john@pc:~/myproject$ git log -p -1
commit a1b2c3d
Author: John <john@example.com>

    Initial commit

diff --git a/index.html b/index.html
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e69de29

john@pc:~/myproject$ git log --stat --oneline
a1b2c3d Initial commit
 index.html | 10 +++++++++
 style.css  |  5 +++++
 2 files changed, 15 insertions(+)

john@pc:~/myproject$ git log --since="2024-01-01" --oneline
b2c3d4e Add README
c3d4e5f Fix typo

john@pc:~/myproject$ git log --author="John" --grep="fix" --oneline
c3d4e5f Fix typo

john@pc:~/myproject$ git show a1b2c3d
commit a1b2c3d
Author: John <john@example.com>
Date:   Thu May 14 10:00:00 2026

    Initial commit

diff --git a/index.html b/index.html
new file mode 100644

john@pc:~/myproject$ git blame index.html a1b2c3d (John 2026-05-14 10:00:00 1)

Hello

b2c3d4e (Jane 2026-05-15 09:00:00 2)

World

john@pc:~/myproject$ git shortlog -s -n
    42  John Doe
    15  Jane Smith
     3  Bob Wilson

commands.name: Diff & Clean
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git diff diff --git a/index.html b/index.html index e69de29..c27a56 100644 --- a/index.html +++ b/index.html @@ -1 +1 @@ -

Old

+

New

john@pc:~/myproject$ git diff --staged
diff --git a/newfile.txt b/newfile.txt
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e69de29

john@pc:~/myproject$ git diff main..feature
...shows all changes between branches
Dry run, shows what would be deleted

john@pc:~/myproject$ git clean -n
Would remove build.log
tmp/

john@pc:~/myproject$ git clean -fd
Removing build.log
Removing tmp/

john@pc:~/myproject$ git clean -fdx
Removing node_modules/
Removing .env

commands.name: .gitignore Patterns
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ echo "node_modules/" >> .gitignore
john@pc:~/myproject$ echo ".env" >> .gitignore
john@pc:~/myproject$ cat .gitignore
node_modules/
.env

john@pc:~/myproject$ git add .gitignore && git commit -m "Add gitignore"
[main 7f8g9h0] Add gitignore
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+)

commands.name: Restore & Revert
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git restore file.txt
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
nothing to commit, working tree clean

john@pc:~/myproject$ git restore --staged file.txt
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
Changes not staged for commit:
	modified:   file.txt

john@pc:~/myproject$ git restore --source=HEAD~2 file.txt
john@pc:~/myproject$ cat file.txt
(older version restored)

john@pc:~/myproject$ git revert HEAD
[main 8i9j0k1] Revert "Add feature"
 1 file changed, 5 deletions(-)

john@pc:~/myproject$ git revert --no-commit HEAD
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
Changes to be committed:
	modified:   file.txt

john@pc:~/myproject$ git revert a1b2c3d..b2c3d4e
[main 0a1b2c3] Revert "Add feature"
[main 1b2c3d4] Revert "Initial commit"

commands.name: Reset
rows: Remove last commit, files stay in staging

john@pc:~/myproject$ git reset --soft HEAD~1
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
Changes to be committed:
	modified:   index.html

john@pc:~/myproject$ git reset HEAD~1
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
Changes not staged for commit:
	modified:   index.html

john@pc:~/myproject$ git reset --hard HEAD~1
HEAD is now at a1b2c3d Initial commit
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
nothing to commit, working tree clean
Discard all local commits, match remote

john@pc:~/myproject$ git reset --hard origin/main
HEAD is now at c3d4e5f Last remote commit

commands.name: Branch Basics
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git branch feature-nav
john@pc:~/myproject$ git branch
* main
  feature-login
  feature-nav

john@pc:~/myproject$ git branch -a
* main
  feature-nav
  remotes/origin/main

john@pc:~/myproject$ git branch -m feature-login login
john@pc:~/myproject$ git branch
* main
  login

john@pc:~/myproject$ git branch -d feature-login
Deleted branch feature-login (was b2c3d4e).

john@pc:~/myproject$ git branch -D feature-login
Deleted branch feature-login (was b2c3d4e).

commands.name: Switch & Checkout
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git checkout feature-nav
Switched to branch 'feature-nav'

john@pc:~/myproject$ git checkout -b new-feature
Switched to a new branch 'new-feature'

john@pc:~/myproject$ git switch main
Switched to branch 'main'

john@pc:~/myproject$ git switch -c new-feature
Switched to a new branch 'new-feature'

john@pc:~/myproject$ git checkout -
Switched to branch 'main'
View commit, not on any branch

john@pc:~/myproject$ git checkout a1b2c3d
Note: switching to 'a1b2c3d'.
You are in 'detached HEAD' state.

commands.name: Merge
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git merge feature-nav
Updating a1b2c3d..d4e5f6g
Fast-forward
 index.html | 5 +++++
 1 file changed, 5 insertions(+)

john@pc:~/myproject$ git merge --no-ff feature-nav
Merge made by the 'ort' strategy.
 index.html | 3 +++
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+)
Combine all branch commits into one

john@pc:~/myproject$ git merge --squash feature-nav
Squash commit -- not updating HEAD
john@pc:~/myproject$ git commit -m "Add feature"
[main 8i9j0k1] Add feature

john@pc:~/myproject$ git merge --abort
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
nothing to commit, working tree clean
(opens visual merge tool like vimdiff, meld, etc.)

commands.name: Rebase
rows: Replays branch on top of main

john@pc:~/myproject$ git rebase main
Current branch feature-x is up to date.

john@pc:~/myproject$ git rebase -i HEAD~3
[detached HEAD 0a1b2c3] Squashed commit
Successfully rebased and updated refs/heads/feature-x.

john@pc:~/myproject$ git rebase --abort
john@pc:~/myproject$ git log --oneline -1
(back to original state)

john@pc:~/myproject$ git rebase --continue
[detached HEAD 1a2b3c4] Fix conflict
Successfully rebased.

commands.name: Cherry Pick & Conflict Resolution
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git cherry-pick a1b2c3d
[main 1a2b3c4] Fix login bug
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

john@pc:~/myproject$ git cherry-pick abc123..def456
[main 2b3c4d5] Change 1
[main 3c4d5e6] Change 2

john@pc:~/myproject$ git cherry-pick -n a1b2c3d
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
Changes to be committed:
	modified:   file.txt

commands.name: Clone & Remote
rows:
john@pc:~$ git clone https://github.com/user/repo.git
Cloning into 'repo'...
remote: Enumerating objects: 45, done.
Receiving objects: 100% (45/45), done.
Resolving deltas: 100% (12/12), done.

john@pc:~$ git clone git@github.com:user/repo.git
Cloning into 'repo'...
remote: Total 45 (delta 12), reused 45
Receiving objects: 100% (45/45), done.

john@pc:~$ git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/user/repo.git
Cloning into 'repo'...
remote: Total 45 (delta 0), reused 0
Receiving objects: 100% (45/45), done.

john@pc:~$ git clone --branch develop https://github.com/user/repo.git
Cloning into 'repo'...
Receiving objects: 100% (45/45), done.

john@pc:~/repo$ git remote -v
origin  https://github.com/user/repo.git (fetch)
origin  https://github.com/user/repo.git (push)

john@pc:~/repo$ git remote add origin https://github.com/user/repo.git
john@pc:~/repo$ git remote -v
origin  https://github.com/user/repo.git (fetch)

john@pc:~/repo$ git remote remove origin
john@pc:~/repo$ git remote -v
(no output, no remotes)

john@pc:~/repo$ git remote rename origin upstream
john@pc:~/repo$ git remote -v
upstream  https://github.com/user/repo.git (fetch)

john@pc:~/repo$ git remote set-url origin git@github.com:user/repo.git
john@pc:~/repo$ git remote -v
origin  git@github.com:user/repo.git (fetch)

commands.name: Push & Pull
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ git push -u origin main
Enumerating objects: 5, done.
Writing objects: 100% (3/3), 1.2 KiB | 1.20 MiB/s, done.
To https://github.com/user/repo.git
 * [new branch]      main -> main
Branch 'main' set up to track remote 'origin/main'.

john@pc:~/repo$ git push origin main
Everything up-to-date

john@pc:~/repo$ git push origin --delete feature-login
To https://github.com/user/repo.git
 - [deleted]         feature-login

john@pc:~/repo$ git push --tags
To https://github.com/user/repo.git
 * [new tag]         v1.0.0 -> v1.0.0
Only force if no one else pushed

john@pc:~/repo$ git push --force-with-lease
 + a1b2c3d...b2c3d4e main -> main (forced)

john@pc:~/repo$ git push -f origin main
 + a1b2c3d...b2c3d4e main -> main (forced)

john@pc:~/repo$ git pull origin main
From https://github.com/user/repo
 * branch main -> FETCH_HEAD
Already up to date.

john@pc:~/repo$ git pull --rebase origin main
Successfully rebased and updated refs/heads/main.

john@pc:~/repo$ git fetch origin
From https://github.com/user/repo
 * [new branch]      main       -> origin/main

commands.name: Tags & Describe
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ git tag v1.0.0
john@pc:~/repo$ git tag
v1.0.0

john@pc:~/repo$ git tag -a v1.0.0 -m "Release v1.0.0"
john@pc:~/repo$ git show v1.0.0 --stat
tag v1.0.0
Tagger: John Doe
Date: Thu May 14 10:00:00 2026

Release v1.0.0

john@pc:~/repo$ git tag -d v1.0.0
Deleted tag 'v1.0.0' (was a1b2c3d)

john@pc:~/repo$ git push origin --delete v1.0.0
To https://github.com/user/repo.git
 - [deleted]         v1.0.0

john@pc:~/repo$ git describe
v1.0.0-5-g1a2b3c4
(5 commits after v1.0.0, hash g1a2b3c4)

commands.name: Stash Operations
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ git stash
Saved working directory and index state WIP on main: a1b2c3d
john@pc:~/repo$ git status
nothing to commit, working tree clean

john@pc:~/repo$ git stash push -m "WIP: login form"
Saved working directory and index state On main: WIP: login form

john@pc:~/repo$ git stash -u
Saved working directory and index state WIP on main: a1b2c3d

john@pc:~/repo$ git stash list
stash@{0}: On main: WIP: login form
stash@{1}: WIP on main: a1b2c3d

john@pc:~/repo$ git stash pop
On branch main
Changes not staged for commit:
	modified:   index.html
dropped refs/stash@{0} (abc123)

john@pc:~/repo$ git stash apply stash@{1}
On branch main
Changes not staged for commit:
	modified:   style.css

john@pc:~/repo$ git stash drop stash@{0}
Dropped stash@{0} (abc123)

john@pc:~/repo$ git stash clear
john@pc:~/repo$ git stash list
(no output, stash is empty)

john@pc:~/repo$ git stash branch feature-x
Switched to a new branch 'feature-x'
On branch feature-x
Changes to be committed:
	modified:   index.html

commands.name: Fork & Upstream Workflow
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ git remote add upstream https://github.com/original/repo.git
john@pc:~/repo$ git remote -v
origin  https://github.com/myuser/repo.git (fetch)
upstream https://github.com/original/repo.git (fetch)

john@pc:~/repo$ git fetch upstream
From https://github.com/original/repo
 * [new branch]      main       -> upstream/main
git checkout main && git merge upstream/main

john@pc:~/repo$ git checkout main && git merge upstream/main
Already on 'main'
Updating a1b2c3d..b2c3d4e
Fast-forward

john@pc:~/repo$ git checkout -b fix-bug
Switched to a new branch 'fix-bug'

john@pc:~/repo$ git push -u origin fix-bug
 * [new branch]      fix-bug -> fix-bug

commands.name: Reflog & Recovery
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ git reflog
a1b2c3d HEAD@{0}: reset: moving to HEAD~1
b2c3d4e HEAD@{1}: commit: Add feature
c3d4e5f HEAD@{2}: commit: Fix header
a1b2c3d HEAD@{3}: commit (initial): Initial commit

john@pc:~/repo$ git reset --hard HEAD@{1}
HEAD is now at b2c3d4e Add feature
john@pc:~/repo$ git log --oneline -1
b2c3d4e Add feature
Control how long reflog keeps history

commands.name: Bisect — Find Bugs
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ git bisect start
john@pc:~/repo$ git bisect bad
john@pc:~/repo$ git bisect good v1.0
Bisecting: 5 revisions left to test after this (roughly 3 steps)

john@pc:~/repo$ git bisect good
abc123 is the first bad commit
commit abc123
Author: John
Date: Mon May 11 2026

    Introduced the bug

john@pc:~/repo$ git bisect reset
Previous HEAD position was abc123...
Switched to branch 'main'

john@pc:~/repo$ git bisect run npm test
... automatically finds the bug commit

commands.name: Submodules & Worktrees
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ git submodule add https://github.com/user/lib.git lib/
Cloning into 'lib'...
done.
john@pc:~/repo$ git status
	new file:   .gitmodules
	new file:   lib

john@pc:~$ git clone --recursive https://github.com/user/repo.git
Cloning into 'repo'...
Submodule 'lib' registered for path 'lib'

john@pc:~/repo$ git submodule update --init --recursive
Submodule path 'lib': checked out 'abc123'

john@pc:~/repo$ git worktree add ../hotfix hotfix
Preparing worktree (new branch 'hotfix')
HEAD is now at a1b2c3d Initial commit

john@pc:~/repo$ git worktree list
/home/john/repo  main
/home/john/hotfix  hotfix

commands.name: Archive, GC & Maintenance
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ git archive --format=zip HEAD > project.zip
john@pc:~/repo$ ls -lh project.zip
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john 12K May 14 10:00 project.zip

john@pc:~/repo$ git gc
Enumerating objects: 45, done.
Counting objects: 100% (45/45), done.
Delta compression using up to 8 threads.
Writing objects: 100% (45/45), done.
Total 45 (delta 12), reused 45 (delta 12)

john@pc:~/repo$ git gc --aggressive
... takes longer but saves more space

john@pc:~/repo$ git fsck
Checking object directories: 100% (256/256), done.
(no output = healthy repo)

john@pc:~/repo$ git count-objects -v
count: 45
size: 120
garbage: 0
pack: 1
size-pack: 45

commands.name: Hooks & GPG Signing
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ ls .git/hooks/
pre-commit.sample  pre-push.sample  post-commit.sample
commit-msg.sample  prepare-commit-msg.sample  update.sample

john@pc:~/repo$ mv .git/hooks/pre-commit.sample .git/hooks/pre-commit
(john edits hooks/pre-commit to customize)

john@pc:~$ git config --global user.signingkey ABCDEF12
(no output)

john@pc:~/repo$ git commit -S -m "Signed commit"
[main 1a2b3c4] Signed commit
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+)

john@pc:~/repo$ git tag -s v1.0.0 -m "Signed tag"
john@pc:~/repo$ git tag -v v1.0.0
object abc123...
type commit
tag v1.0.0
gpg: Signature made ... using RSA key

john@pc:~/repo$ git log --show-signature -1
gpg: Signature made Thu May 14 2026
gpg: Good signature from "John Doe"
commit a1b2c3d

commands.name: Common Fixes
rows: git status (first step for any issue)

john@pc:~/repo$ git status
On branch main
Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.
nothing to commit, working tree clean
Fix: run inside a Git repo
Run git init or cd to a repo
fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git
error: Your local changes would be overwritten by merge.
Edit file, remove markers, git add

Auto-merging file.txt
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in file.txt
Automatic merge failed; fix conflicts and commit.

! [rejected] main -> main (fetch first)
error: failed to push some refs
hint: Updates were rejected because the remote contains work
Your branch is ahead of 'origin/main' by 2 commits

On branch main
Your branch is ahead of 'origin/main' by 2 commits.
  (use "git push" to publish your local commits)

You are in 'detached HEAD' state.
If you want to create a new branch:
  git switch -c new-branch-name

hint: You have divergent branches and need to specify how to reconcile them.
hint: git pull --rebase

commands.name: Best Practices
rows: Subject line (<50 chars) + blank line + body
